summary: Comprehensive backend + frontend testing of The Morning Dispatch. All 11 backend pytest cases pass including real Claude Sonnet 4.5 LLM brief generation, override-based regeneration, and evening-close persistence. Frontend renders the Bharat/Lightspeed Knowledge Base §5 brief perfectly, Cmd+K Demo Console opens, scenario clicks trigger regeneration overlay, and /evening + /mockups/slack + /mockups/email all load with required data-testids.

issues: Scenario items in the Demo Console do not expose a data-testid prefix like 'scenario-*' — only generic buttons are queryable. Optional: add data-testid='scenario-<id>' to each scenario for cleaner automated testing.

action_items: Optional polish: add data-testid='scenario-<id>' to each Demo Console scenario item (currently only generic buttons inside [data-testid=demo-console]). No functional impact.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py uses a module-level _Store class for in-memory persistence — fine for hackathon demo per PRD §6 scope, but not thread-safe / process-safe (acceptable for single-worker uvicorn).
generate_brief raises HTTPException(500) wrapping all exceptions — could leak internal error details to clients; consider trimming for production but OK for demo.
CORS is wide-open ('*') with allow_credentials=True — browsers will reject credentialed wildcard, but no cookies/auth here so it works. Demo-acceptable.
seed_data_creation: Test posted an evening-close payload with TEST_ prefixed entries (in-memory only, no DB persistence per PRD).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend pytest at /app/backend/tests/test_dispatch.py covers all 8 endpoints incl. LLM generation (Claude Sonnet 4.5, ~26s total for full suite). Brief LLM output reliably contains 'bharat'/'lightspeed' in oneThing matching KB §5. Override path tested: removing email-1 causes oneThing to no longer mention Bharat. Frontend uses NEXT_PUBLIC_BACKEND_URL with the same value as REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL. Use page.keyboard.press('Meta+k') to open Demo Console (works). Scenario buttons currently lack scenario-specific data-testids.
